title: Charmed Life - Chapter 8 (Fragment 1)
text:
Chapter Five

5

G
 WENDOLEN REFUSED to tell Cat what she was going to do. This meant that Cat had rather a melancholy time. After a wholesome lunch of turnips and boiled mutton, they had lessons again. After that, Gwendolen ran hastily away and would not let Cat come with her. Cat did not know what to do.

“Would you care to come out and play?” Roger asked.

Cat looked at him and saw that he was just being polite. “No, thank you,” he said politely. He was forced to wander around the gardens on his own. There was a wood lower down, full of horse chestnuts, but they were not nearly ripe. As Cat was halfheartedly staring up into one, he saw there was a tree house in it, about halfway up. This was more like it. Cat was just about to climb up to it when he heard voices and saw Julia’s skirt flutter among the leaves. So that was no good. It was Julia and Roger’s private tree house, and they were in it.

Cat wandered away again. He came to the lawn, and there was Gwendolen, crouching under one of the cedars, very busy digging a small hole.

“What are you doing?” said Cat.

“Go away,” said Gwendolen.

Cat went away. He was sure what Gwendolen was doing was witchcraft and had to do with teaching Chrestomanci a lesson, but it was no good asking Gwendolen when she was being this secretive. Cat had to wait. He waited through another terrifying dinner, and then through a long, long evening. Gwendolen locked herself in her room after dinner and told him to go away when he knocked.

Next morning, Cat woke up early and hurried to the nearest of his three windows. He saw at once what Gwendolen had been doing. The lawn was ruined. It was not a smooth stretch of green velvet any longer. It was a mass of molehills. As far as Cat could see in both directions, there were little green mounds, little heaps of raw earth, long lines of raw earth and long green furrows of raised grass. There must have been an army of moles at work on it all night. About a dozen gardeners were standing in a gloomy huddle, scratching their heads over it.

Cat threw on his clothes and dashed downstairs.

Gwendolen was leaning out of her window in her frilly cotton nightdress, glowing with pride. “Look at that!” she said to Cat. “Isn’t it marvelous! There’s acres of it too. It took me hours yesterday evening to make sure it was all spoiled. That will make Chrestomanci think a bit!”

Cat was sure it would. He did not know how much a huge stretch of turf like that would cost to replace, but he suspected it was a great deal. He was afraid Gwendolen would be in really bad trouble.

But, to his astonishment, nobody so much as mentioned the lawn. Euphemia came in a minute later, but all she said was, “You’ll both be late for your breakfast again.” Roger and Julia said nothing at all. They silently accepted the marmalade and Cat’s knife when he passed them over, but the sole thing either of them said was when Julia dropped Cat’s knife and picked it up again, all fluffy. She said, “Bother!” And when Mr. Saunders called them through for lessons, the only things he talked about were what he was teaching them. Cat decided that nobody knew Gwendolen had caused the moles. They could have no idea what a strong witch she was.

There were no lessons after lunch that day. Mr. Saunders explained that they always had Wednesday afternoons off. And at lunchtime, every molehill had gone. When they looked out of the playroom window, the lawn was like a sheet of velvet again.

vocabulary.definition: to surprise greatly
vocabulary.example: To his astonishment, nobody so much as mentioned the lawn.

vocabulary.definition: great surprise or amazement
vocabulary.example: But, to his astonishment, nobody so much as mentioned the lawn.

vocabulary.definition: stooping or bending low with the limbs close to the body
vocabulary.example: Gwendolen, crouching under one of the cedars, very busy digging.

vocabulary.definition: a mild or indirect word or expression substituted for one considered harsh
vocabulary.example: Euphemia came in and said, “You’ll both be late for your breakfast again.”

vocabulary.definition: to wave or flap quickly and lightly
vocabulary.example: Julia’s skirt flutter among the leaves.

vocabulary.definition: a long narrow trench made in the ground
vocabulary.example: Long green furrows of raised grass.

vocabulary.definition: dark or poorly lit; depressing or dreary
vocabulary.example: Gardeners were standing in a gloomy huddle.

vocabulary.definition: shining with warmth or light; expressing pride or happiness
vocabulary.example: Gwendolen was glowing with pride.

vocabulary.definition: without enthusiasm or energy
vocabulary.example: Cat was halfheartedly staring up into one.

vocabulary.definition: with excessive speed or urgency; hurriedly
vocabulary.example: Gwendolen ran hastily away and would not let Cat come with her.

vocabulary.definition: a close group or gathering
vocabulary.example: Gardeners were standing in a gloomy huddle.

vocabulary.definition: something wonderful or astonishing
vocabulary.example: Look at that! Isn't it marvelous!

vocabulary.definition: causing great wonder; extraordinary
vocabulary.example: Isn't it marvelous!

vocabulary.definition: a feeling of deep sadness or gloom
vocabulary.example: Cat had rather a melancholy time.

vocabulary.definition: a rounded mass projecting above a surface
vocabulary.example: There were little green mounds, little heaps of raw earth.

vocabulary.definition: showing good manners and respect for others
vocabulary.example: He was just being polite.

vocabulary.definition: a feeling of deep pleasure from one's achievements
vocabulary.example: She was glowing with pride.

vocabulary.definition: declined to do something
vocabulary.example: Wendolen refused to tell Cat what she was going to do.

vocabulary.definition: to put something new instead of something old or damaged
vocabulary.example: How much a huge stretch of turf like that would cost to replace.

vocabulary.definition: to rub a surface with fingernails or claws
vocabulary.example: They were scratching their heads over it.

vocabulary.definition: inclined to conceal feelings or information
vocabulary.example: Gwendolen was being this secretive.

vocabulary.definition: without making any sound
vocabulary.example: They silently accepted the marmalade.

vocabulary.definition: causing extreme fear
vocabulary.example: He waited through another terrifying dinner.

vocabulary.definition: surface layer of earth with grass
vocabulary.example: A huge stretch of turf like that would cost to replace.

vocabulary.definition: promoting health or well-being
vocabulary.example: After a wholesome lunch of turnips and boiled mutton, they had lessons again.

vocabulary.definition: the practice of magical skills, spells, and abilities
vocabulary.example: He was sure what Gwendolen was doing was witchcraft.
